You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.1714 (commit: 7c30e125).

## Issue to Resolve

Update to Anki 23.12 backend
The Anki update includes some significant changes to the FSRS algorithm. So, for best results, all reviews should be done on apps with the same codebase.

(There are no "breaking" changes, but still ...)

Now that the 23.12 release is public, the backend code of AnkiDroid should be updated as soon as possible.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I/we get notifications for anki releases, we are aware of them for what it's worth
I was almost certain that you guys get notifications for the releases, but I still created this issue because the 23.12 update changes the scheduler code, which means that doing reviews on apps with different backend versions can lead to some unwanted behaviour. So, quickly updating to this version is somewhat more important.

After this release, for several months, there are no expected changes to the FSRS scheduler code. So, I would not urge for a very rapid update to the new backend version for those releases.
Also, 23.12 introduced some important fixes. From release notes:

- When cards are rescheduled multiple times without reviews, extra entries will not be added to the review log.
- Fix some bugs in sorting by FSRS retrievability.
- Recompute FSRS memory state when cards moved into different deck.
- Added a new v3 option to limit child decks by their parent, when clicking on the child.
- Fix filtered deck ordering of intraday learning cards and reviews
- Fix relative overdueness order error in filtered deck.
The new backend can't be simply dropped in. Some changes will need to be made to AnkiDroid:

- The filtered deck screen will need to support customizing the separate preview delays for again/hard & good buttons
- The auto advance feature will need to switch to using the settings in the current card's deck options
> The new backend can't be simply dropped in.

Yes, I agree. All that I have to say is this update should take a slightly higher priority than normal.
Another issue I didn't mention above: Anki displays various options when importing an .apkg file, which AnkiDroid is currently not doing. As a temporary workaround to get things to compile when switching to the 23.12 backend, you can hard-code the argument change:

```diff
diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
index 07ec516536..bb272bd17b 100644
--- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
+++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
@@ -17,6 +17,7 @@
 package com.ichi2.libanki

 import anki.import_export.ExportLimit
+import anki.import_export.exportAnkiPackageOptions
 import anki.search.SearchNode
 import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend

@@ -107,7 +108,13 @@ fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
     limit: ExportLimit,
     legacy: Boolean = true
 ) {
-    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
+    val options = exportAnkiPackageOptions {
+        this.withScheduling = withScheduling
+        this.withMedia = withMedia
+        this.legacy = legacy
+        this.withDeckConfigs = true
+    }
+    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
 }

 fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
diff --git a/build.gradle b/build.gradle
index aeafc762f8..2057d8a32f 100644
--- a/build.gradle
+++ b/build.gradle
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@ buildscript {
     ext.kotlin_version = '1.9.21'
     ext.lint_version = '31.1.1'
     ext.acra_version = '5.11.3'
-    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.32-anki23.10.1'
+    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.34-anki23.12'
     ext.hamcrest_version = '2.2'
     ext.junit_version = '5.10.1'
     ext.coroutines_version = '1.7.3'
```

A proper fix will mean using the import-anki-package.html page. Currently, for some unknown reason, it is not being included in the backend builds, and it could use the help of someone knowledgeable in Gradle to debug.

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android-Backend/pull/339

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/assets/card_template.html`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/BackendImporting.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiPackageImporterFragment.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiServer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/PostRequestHandler.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/PreviewerViewModel.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt`
- `build.gradle`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

27â†’class AnkiPackageImporterFragment : PageFragment() {
28â†’    override val title: String
29â†’        get() = resources.getString(R.string.menu_import)
30â†’    override val pageName: String
31â†’        get() = "import-page"
32â†’    override lateinit var webViewClient: PageWebViewClient
33â†’    override var webChromeClient: PageChromeClient = PageChromeClient()
34â†’    private lateinit var backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
35â†’
36â†’    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
37â†’        // the back callback is only enabled when import is running and showing progress
38â†’        backCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {
39â†’            override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
40â†’                CollectionManager.getBackend().setWantsAbort()
41â†’                // once triggered the callback is not needed as the import process can't be resumed
42â†’                remove()
43â†’            }
44â†’        }
45â†’        val path = arguments?.getString(ARG_FILE_PATH)
46â†’            ?: throw IllegalStateException("No path provided for apkg package to import")
47â†’        webViewClient = AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(path, backCallback)
48â†’        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
49â†’        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, backCallback)
50â†’    }
51â†’
52â†’    class AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(
53â†’        private val path: String,
54â†’        private val backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
55â†’    ) : PageWebViewClient() {
56â†’        /**
57â†’         * Ideally, to handle the state of the back callback, we would just need to check for
58â†’         * `/latestProgress` calls followed by one `/importDone` call. However there are some extra
59â†’         * calls to `/latestProgress` AFTER `/importDone` and this property keeps track of this.
60â†’         */
61â†’        private var isDone = false
62â†’
63â†’        override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
64â†’            val params = """{ type: "json_file", path: "$path"}"""
65â†’            // https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/main/ts/import-page/index.ts
66â†’            view!!.evaluateJavascript("anki.setupImportPage($params);$hideShowButtonCss;") {
67â†’                super.onPageFinished(view, url)
68â†’            }
69â†’        }
70â†’
71â†’        override fun onLoadResource(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
72â†’            super.onLoadResource(view, url)
73â†’            backCallback.isEnabled = when {
74â†’                url == null -> false
75â†’                url.endsWith("latestProgress") && !isDone -> true
76â†’                url.endsWith("importDone") -> {
77â†’                    isDone = true // import was done so disable any back callback changes after this call
78â†’                    false
79â†’                }
80â†’                else -> false
81â†’            }
82â†’        }
83â†’    }
84â†’
85â†’    companion object {
86â†’        private const val ARG_FILE_PATH = "arg_file_path"
87â†’
88â†’        fun getIntent(context: Context, filePath: String): Intent {
89â†’            val args = bundleOf(ARG_FILE_PATH to filePath)
90â†’            return SingleFragmentActivity.getIntent(context, AnkiPackageImporterFragment::class, args)
91â†’        }
92â†’    }
93â†’}

 18â†’package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 19â†’
 20â†’import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD
 21â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
 22â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
 23â†’import timber.log.Timber
 24â†’import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
 25â†’
 26â†’const val PORT = 0
 27â†’// const val PORT = 40001
 28â†’
 29â†’// local debugging:
 30â†’// ~/Local/Android/Sdk/platform-tools/adb forward tcp:40001 tcp:40001
 31â†’
 32â†’open class AnkiServer(
 33â†’    private val postHandler: PostRequestHandler
 34â†’) : NanoHTTPD(LOCALHOST, PORT) {
 35â†’
 36â†’    fun baseUrl(): String {
 37â†’        return "http://$LOCALHOST:$listeningPort/"
 38â†’    }
 39â†’
 40â†’    // it's faster to serve local files without GZip. see 'page render' in logs
 41â†’    // This also removes 'W/System: A resource failed to call end.'
 42â†’    override fun useGzipWhenAccepted(r: Response?) = false
 43â†’
 44â†’    override fun serve(session: IHTTPSession): Response {
 45â†’        val uri = session.uri
 46â†’        val mime = getMimeFromUri(uri)
 47â†’
 48â†’        if (session.method == Method.GET) {
 49â†’            val resourcePath = "web$uri"
 50â†’            val stream = this.javaClass.classLoader!!.getResourceAsStream(resourcePath)
 51â†’            Timber.d("GET: Requested %s (%s), found? %b", uri, resourcePath, stream != null)
 52â†’            return newChunkedResponse(Response.Status.OK, mime, stream)
 53â†’        }
 54â†’
 55â†’        if (session.method == Method.POST) {
 56â†’            Timber.d("POST: Requested %s", uri)
 57â†’            val inputBytes = getSessionBytes(session)
 58â†’            return buildResponse {
 59â†’                postHandler.handlePostRequest(uri, inputBytes)
 60â†’            }
 61â†’        }
 62â†’        return newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 63â†’    }
 64â†’
 65â†’    private fun buildResponse(
 66â†’        block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> ByteArray
 67â†’    ): Response {
 68â†’        return try {
 69â†’            val data = runBlocking {
 70â†’                block()
 71â†’            }
 72â†’            newChunkedResponse(data)
 73â†’        } catch (exc: Exception) {
 74â†’            newChunkedResponse(exc.localizedMessage?.encodeToByteArray(), status = Response.Status.INTERNAL_ERROR)
 75â†’        }
 76â†’    }
 77â†’
 78â†’    companion object {
 79â†’        const val LOCALHOST = "127.0.0.1"
 80â†’
 81â†’        /** Common prefix used on Anki requests */
 82â†’        const val ANKI_PREFIX = "/_anki/"
 83â†’        const val ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX = "/jsapi/"
 84â†’
 85â†’        fun getMimeFromUri(uri: String): String {
 86â†’            return when (uri.substringAfterLast(".")) {
 87â†’                "ico" -> "image/x-icon"
 88â†’                "css" -> "text/css"
 89â†’                "js" -> "text/javascript"
 90â†’                "html" -> "text/html"
 91â†’                else -> "application/binary"
 92â†’            }
 93â†’        }
 94â†’
 95â†’        fun getSessionBytes(session: IHTTPSession): ByteArray {
 96â†’            val contentLength = session.headers["content-length"]!!.toInt()
 97â†’            val bytes = ByteArray(contentLength)
 98â†’            session.inputStream.read(bytes, 0, contentLength)
 99â†’            return bytes
100â†’        }
101â†’
102â†’        fun newChunkedResponse(
103â†’            data: ByteArray?,
104â†’            mimeType: String = "application/binary",
105â†’            status: Response.IStatus = Response.Status.OK
106â†’        ): Response {
107â†’            return if (data == null) {
108â†’                newFixedLengthResponse(null)
109â†’            } else {
110â†’                newChunkedResponse(status, mimeType, ByteArrayInputStream(data))
111â†’            }
112â†’        }
113â†’    }
114â†’}
115â†’

 23â†’/**
 24â†’ * (Maybe) create a colpkg backup, while keeping the collection open. If the
 25â†’ * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 26â†’ * and this routine will return false.
 27â†’ *
 28â†’ * There must not be an active transaction.
 29â†’ *
 30â†’ * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 31â†’ * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 32â†’ * to check for success.
 33â†’ *
 34â†’ * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 35â†’ *
 36â†’ */
 37â†’fun Collection.createBackup(
 38â†’    backupFolder: String,
 39â†’    force: Boolean,
 40â†’    waitForCompletion: Boolean
 41â†’): Boolean {
 42â†’    return backend.createBackup(
 43â†’        backupFolder = backupFolder,
 44â†’        force = force,
 45â†’        waitForCompletion = waitForCompletion
 46â†’    )
 47â†’}
 48â†’
 49â†’/**
 50â†’ * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 51â†’ * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 52â†’ * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.
 53â†’ */
 54â†’fun Collection.awaitBackupCompletion() {
 55â†’    backend.awaitBackupCompletion()
 56â†’}

 58â†’/**
 59â†’ * Replace the collection file with the one in the provided .colpkg file.
 60â†’ * The collection must be already closed, and must be opened afterwards.
 61â†’ * */

 63â†’    backend: Backend,
 64â†’    colPath: String,
 65â†’    colpkgPath: String
 66â†’) {
 67â†’    backend.importCollectionPackage(
 68â†’        colPath = colPath,
 69â†’        backupPath = colpkgPath,
 70â†’        mediaFolder = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media"),
 71â†’        mediaDb = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media.db")
 72â†’    )
 73â†’}

 76â†’ * Export the collection into a .colpkg file.
 77â†’ * If legacy=false, a file targeting Anki 2.1.50+ is created. It compresses better and is faster to
 78â†’ * create, but older clients can not read it.
 79â†’ */

 81â†’    outPath: String,
 82â†’    includeMedia: Boolean,
 83â†’    legacy: Boolean = true

 88â†’        includeMedia = includeMedia,

 90â†’    )

The changes have been successfully implemented. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. Updated Backend Version (`/project/build.gradle`)
- Changed `ankidroid_backend_version` from `'0.1.32-anki23.10.1'` to `'0.1.34-anki23.12'`
- This updates the Anki backend to version 23.12 which includes important FSRS algorithm fixes

### 2. Updated `exportAnkiPackage` Function (`/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt`)
- Added import for `anki.import_export.exportAnkiPackageOptions`
- Modified the `exportAnkiPackage` function to use the new API that requires an `ExportAnkiPackageOptions` object instead of individual boolean parameters
- The new implementation creates an options object with:
  - `withScheduling` - preserves scheduling information
  - `withMedia` - includes media files
  - `legacy` - compatibility mode for older Anki versions
  - `withDeckConfigs = true` - includes deck configurations (hardcoded as suggested in the issue)

These changes address the issue requirements:
- âś… Backend updated to Anki 23.12
- âś… `exportAnkiPackage` API updated to use the new options-based signature
- âś… The temporary workaround of hard-coding `withDeckConfigs = true` is implemented as suggested in the issue discussion

The changes are minimal and targeted, ensuring compatibility with the new backend while maintaining existing functionality.

 18â†’package com.ichi2.anki.pages
 19â†’
 20â†’import fi.iki.elonen.NanoHTTPD
 21â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineScope
 22â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.runBlocking
 23â†’import timber.log.Timber
 24â†’import java.io.ByteArrayInputStream
 25â†’
 26â†’const val PORT = 0
 27â†’// const val PORT = 40001
 28â†’
 29â†’// local debugging:
 30â†’// ~/Local/Android/Sdk/platform-tools/adb forward tcp:40001 tcp:40001
 31â†’
 32â†’open class AnkiServer(
 33â†’    private val postHandler: PostRequestHandler
 34â†’) : NanoHTTPD(LOCALHOST, PORT) {
 35â†’
 36â†’    fun baseUrl(): String {
 37â†’        return "http://$LOCALHOST:$listeningPort/"
 38â†’    }
 39â†’
 40â†’    // it's faster to serve local files without GZip. see 'page render' in logs
 41â†’    // This also removes 'W/System: A resource failed to call end.'
 42â†’    override fun useGzipWhenAccepted(r: Response?) = false
 43â†’
 44â†’    override fun serve(session: IHTTPSession): Response {
 45â†’        val uri = session.uri
 46â†’        val mime = getMimeFromUri(uri)
 47â†’
 48â†’        if (session.method == Method.GET) {
 49â†’            val resourcePath = "web$uri"
 50â†’            val stream = this.javaClass.classLoader!!.getResourceAsStream(resourcePath)
 51â†’            Timber.d("GET: Requested %s (%s), found? %b", uri, resourcePath, stream != null)
 52â†’            return newChunkedResponse(Response.Status.OK, mime, stream)
 53â†’        }
 54â†’
 55â†’        if (session.method == Method.POST) {
 56â†’            Timber.d("POST: Requested %s", uri)
 57â†’            val inputBytes = getSessionBytes(session)
 58â†’            return buildResponse {
 59â†’                postHandler.handlePostRequest(uri, inputBytes)
 60â†’            }
 61â†’        }
 62â†’        return newFixedLengthResponse(null)
 63â†’    }
 64â†’
 65â†’    private fun buildResponse(
 66â†’        block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> ByteArray
 67â†’    ): Response {
 68â†’        return try {
 69â†’            val data = runBlocking {
 70â†’                block()
 71â†’            }
 72â†’            newChunkedResponse(data)
 73â†’        } catch (exc: Exception) {
 74â†’            newChunkedResponse(exc.localizedMessage?.encodeToByteArray(), status = Response.Status.INTERNAL_ERROR)
 75â†’        }
 76â†’    }
 77â†’
 78â†’    companion object {
 79â†’        const val LOCALHOST = "127.0.0.1"
 80â†’
 81â†’        /** Common prefix used on Anki requests */
 82â†’        const val ANKI_PREFIX = "/_anki/"
 83â†’        const val ANKIDROID_JS_PREFIX = "/jsapi/"
 84â†’
 85â†’        fun getMimeFromUri(uri: String): String {
 86â†’            return when (uri.substringAfterLast(".")) {
 87â†’                "ico" -> "image/x-icon"
 88â†’                "css" -> "text/css"
 89â†’                "js" -> "text/javascript"
 90â†’                "html" -> "text/html"
 91â†’                else -> "application/binary"
 92â†’            }
 93â†’        }
 94â†’
 95â†’        fun getSessionBytes(session: IHTTPSession): ByteArray {
 96â†’            val contentLength = session.headers["content-length"]!!.toInt()
 97â†’            val bytes = ByteArray(contentLength)
 98â†’            session.inputStream.read(bytes, 0, contentLength)
 99â†’            return bytes
100â†’        }
101â†’
102â†’        fun newChunkedResponse(
103â†’            data: ByteArray?,
104â†’            mimeType: String = "application/binary",
105â†’            status: Response.IStatus = Response.Status.OK
106â†’        ): Response {
107â†’            return if (data == null) {
108â†’                newFixedLengthResponse(null)
109â†’            } else {
110â†’                newChunkedResponse(status, mimeType, ByteArrayInputStream(data))
111â†’            }
112â†’        }
113â†’    }
114â†’}

27â†’class AnkiPackageImporterFragment : PageFragment() {
28â†’    override val title: String
29â†’        get() = resources.getString(R.string.menu_import)
30â†’    override val pageName: String
31â†’        get() = "import-page"
32â†’    override lateinit var webViewClient: PageWebViewClient
33â†’    override var webChromeClient: PageChromeClient = PageChromeClient()
34â†’    private lateinit var backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
35â†’
36â†’    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
37â†’        // the back callback is only enabled when import is running and showing progress
38â†’        backCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(false) {
39â†’            override fun handleOnBackPressed() {
40â†’                CollectionManager.getBackend().setWantsAbort()
41â†’                // once triggered the callback is not needed as the import process can't be resumed
42â†’                remove()
43â†’            }
44â†’        }
45â†’        val path = arguments?.getString(ARG_FILE_PATH)
46â†’            ?: throw IllegalStateException("No path provided for apkg package to import")
47â†’        webViewClient = AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(path, backCallback)
48â†’        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
49â†’        requireActivity().onBackPressedDispatcher.addCallback(this, backCallback)
50â†’    }
51â†’
52â†’    class AnkiPackageImporterWebViewClient(
53â†’        private val path: String,
54â†’        private val backCallback: OnBackPressedCallback
55â†’    ) : PageWebViewClient() {
56â†’        /**
57â†’         * Ideally, to handle the state of the back callback, we would just need to check for
58â†’         * `/latestProgress` calls followed by one `/importDone` call. However there are some extra
59â†’         * calls to `/latestProgress` AFTER `/importDone` and this property keeps track of this.
60â†’         */
61â†’        private var isDone = false
62â†’
63â†’        override fun onPageFinished(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
64â†’            val params = """{ type: "json_file", path: "$path"}"""
65â†’            // https://github.com/ankitects/anki/blob/main/ts/import-page/index.ts
66â†’            view!!.evaluateJavascript("anki.setupImportPage($params);$hideShowButtonCss;") {
67â†’                super.onPageFinished(view, url)
68â†’            }
69â†’        }
70â†’
71â†’        override fun onLoadResource(view: WebView?, url: String?) {
72â†’            super.onLoadResource(view, url)
73â†’            backCallback.isEnabled = when {
74â†’                url == null -> false
75â†’                url.endsWith("latestProgress") && !isDone -> true
76â†’                url.endsWith("importDone") -> {
77â†’                    isDone = true // import was done so disable any back callback changes after this call
78â†’                    false
79â†’                }
80â†’                else -> false
81â†’            }
82â†’        }
83â†’    }
84â†’
85â†’    companion object {
86â†’        private const val ARG_FILE_PATH = "arg_file_path"
87â†’
88â†’        fun getIntent(context: Context, filePath: String): Intent {
89â†’            val args = bundleOf(ARG_FILE_PATH to filePath)
90â†’            return SingleFragmentActivity.getIntent(context, AnkiPackageImporterFragment::class, args)
91â†’        }
92â†’    }
93â†’}
